entry.title: Re: After working as a SAP SD Professional, should I pursue an MBA or a master's degree?
entry.content:
Congratulations on completing your SAP S/4 Hana Sales and Distribution certification and gaining one year of experience in SAP SD! Pursuing an MBA or master's degree can indeed open up new opportunities and enhance your expertise. Here are some considerations:

1. Choosing an MBA or Master's Program:

  • Look for programs that specialize in Business Analytics, Information Systems, or Operations Management. Some top business schools also offer specialized courses in SAP or ERP systems.
  • Research universities abroad that have strong ties with industry and a good track record in placing graduates in relevant roles.

2. Enhancing SAP SD Expertise:

  • An MBA or master's program can provide you with a broader understanding of business strategy, leadership, and management, complementing your technical SAP skills.
  • Some programs offer electives or concentrations in Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems, giving you a deeper understanding of the business aspects of SAP SD.

3. Professional Options:

  • With an MBA or master's degree, you could explore roles such as SAP Consultant, Business Analyst, Project Manager, or even move into managerial positions.
  • The combination of technical SAP skills and business acumen can make you a valuable asset in roles that require both IT expertise and strategic decision-making.

4. Utilizing SAP SD Experience in Your Studies:

  • Share your real-world SAP SD experiences during class discussions, case studies, and projects. This practical knowledge can enrich classroom learning for you and your peers.
  • Consider a thesis or capstone project related to optimizing SAP SD processes within a business context.

5. Online or Part-Time Programs:

  • Many reputable universities offer online or part-time MBA or master's programs. These can be ideal for working professionals, allowing you to continue gaining experience while pursuing higher education.

Remember to thoroughly research programs, consider their alumni network, and explore any internship or industry placement opportunities they offer.

Lastly, consult with career counselors, alumni, and professionals who have taken a similar career path for personalized advice based on your goals and aspirations.

entry.title: Re: How to use UDF values in FSM HTML report?
entry.content:
Okay, so it took me a while, but @DAcker I've found some sort of generic solution.

In template.js add for getMileagesTableDescriptor new column:

{
    headerKey: 'Mileage_Udf_Notes_L', udfValue: 'MILEAGE_NOTES',
}

then in template.html in genericTableTemplate for <td> entry:

{{else if udfValue}}
    {{#each data-row.udfValues}}
        {{#compare meta.name "==" column.udfValue}}
            {{value}}
        {{/compare}}
    {{/each}}

 Nów, how it works:

- when generating generic table it will check if attribute "udfValue" in column is set. If yes, it iterates through all udfValues for this row and checks if meta.name matches the attribute value. This way you can use it in any table. 

Full code:
genericTableTemplate

<script id="genericTableTemplate" type="text/x-handlebars-template">
    {{#if dataGroups}}
        {{#gt dataGroups.length 0}}
            <section class="generic-table">
                <h2>{{ localize titleKey }}</h2>
                <table>
                    <thead>
                    <tr>
                        {{#each columns}}
                            <th>{{ localize headerKey }}</th>
                        {{/each}}
                    </tr>
                    </thead>

                    <tbody>
                    {{#each dataGroups as |data-group|}}
                        {{#each . as |data-row|}}
                            <tr>
                                {{#each ../../columns as |column|}}
                                    <td>
                                        {{#if propertyName}}
                                            {{formatValue (get propertyName data-row) column }}

                                        {{else if aggregate}}
                                            {{formatValue (aggregateValues data-row column) column }}

                                        {{else if udfValue}}
                                            {{#each data-row.udfValues}}
                                                {{#compare meta.name "==" column.udfValue}}
                                                    {{value}}
                                                {{/compare}}
                                            {{/each}}

                                        {{/if}}
                                    </td>
                                {{/each}}
                            </tr>
                        {{/each}}
                        <tr>
                            {{#each ../columns as |column|}}
                                <td>
                                    {{#if column.footer}}
                                        <div class="column-footer">
                                            {{#if column.footer.formatFooter}}
                                                {{formatValue (callFunction column.footer.valueProvider ../this/this) column}}
                                            {{else}}
                                                {{callFunction column.footer.valueProvider ../this/this}}
                                            {{/if}}
                                        </div>
                                    {{/if}}
                                </td>
                            {{/each}}
                        </tr>
                    {{/each}}
                    </tbody>
                </table>
            </section>
        {{/gt}}
    {{/if}}
</script>

getMileagesTableDescriptor

const getMileagesTableDescriptor = (mileages, localization) => ({
    titleKey: 'EntityPluralName_Mileage',
    columns: [
        {
            headerKey: 'Mileage_StartDate', propertyName: 'travelStartDateTime', templateId: 'dateTimeTemplate', templateOptions: {formatString: 'L'},
            footer: {
                valueProvider: _ => localization.total,
                formatFooter: false
            }
        },
        {headerKey: 'ExpenseMaterialMileage_Detail_CreatePerson_L', aggregate: ['createPerson.firstName', 'createPerson.lastName'], operator: 'concat'},
        {headerKey: 'Mileage_Detail_From_L', propertyName: 'source'},
        {headerKey: 'Mileage_Detail_To_L', propertyName: 'destination'},
        {
            headerKey: 'Mileage_Detail_Distance_L', propertyName: 'distance', templateId: 'numberTemplate',
            footer: {
                valueProvider: mileages => mileages.map(mileage => mileage.distance).reduce((acc, current) => acc + current, 0),
                formatFooter: true
            }
        },
        {
            headerKey: 'Mileage_Detail_TravelDuration_L', aggregate: ['travelEndDateTime', 'travelStartDateTime'], operator: 'difference', templateId: 'durationTemplate',
            footer: {
                valueProvider: mileages => mileages.filter(mileage => mileage.travelStartDateTime && mileage.travelEndDateTime).map(mileage => mileage.travelEndDateTime - mileage.travelStartDateTime).reduce((acc, current) => acc + current, 0),
                formatFooter: true
            }
        },
        {headerKey: 'Mileage_Udf_Notes_L', udfValue: 'MILEAGE_NOTES'}
        ],
    dataGroups: mileages && mileages.length ? [mileages] : []
});

 

Although for me column header is not displayed even I've added key to localization file, but I will leave it up to you.

entry.title: SAP Note 3223858
entry.content:
I am searching the SAP Note 3223858 in this site, however I am unable to get the detail document.  

Help please!

SAP Note 3223858 - BSI: Filing status synchronization with TaxFactory 11.0 delivers the BSI Filing Status Synchronization (BSIFS) configuration option. And you can use this configuration to activate (ON) or deactivate (OFF) the usage of the BSI Filing Status (T5UTKZ) as source of information for valid filing statuses, in the Customer values for configuration options (V_T5F99K2) table view.

Navigating the complex landscape of innovation can be daunting. It's not just about embracing the latest trends; it's about ensuring that innovation aligns with the needs of your people, is economically viable, and technically feasible. Adding AI into the mix further complicates the equation, as the possibilities it offers are both vast and sometimes overwhelming.

You might find yourself asking, "How can I find the right balance between these factors, including the integration of AI, to create tangible value?"

While there are numerous approaches to this question, our stand-out solution is:

SAP AppHaus’ Human-Centred Approach to Innovation, coupled with its Generative AI Explore Workshop.

Why focus on human-centred design? Often, the most effective way to improve processes is to start by understanding the needs and pain points of the people who actually perform the work. While it may seem intuitive to prioritize certain aspects of innovation, it's only through engaging with end-users that you can truly identify where innovation is most needed.

But where to begin? How do other people and organizations tackle this innovation pressure? This is where thought leaders come into play—individuals who possess the expertise, insights, and innovative ideas to guide you in the right direction.

On March 13, 2024, you’ll have the opportunity to hear from Andreas Hauser, Senior Vice President and Head of the SAP AppHaus Network. With a wealth of experience in orchestrating innovation projects worldwide run by the global SAP AppHaus Network teams, Andreas collaborates closely with experts across diverse fields, gaining insights which he uses to enrich projects with customers.

Join and listen to Andreas share his invaluable insights, and actively engage in dialogue. Post your questions, share your perspectives, and glean insights from a diverse community of innovators.

By the end of the session, you'll emerge with a deeper understanding of today's opportunities, armed with strategies for leveraging SAP's Business AI with SAP Business Technology Platform, in conjunction with the SAP AppHaus Network to propel your innovation journey forward. You will get actionable insights, practical tips, and exclusive access to valuable resources that will empower you to navigate the ever-evolving landscape of innovation with confidence.

entry.title: Re: SAP Note 3223858
entry.content:
Hi,

 

what exactly are you looking for? You cannot see what is written in the solution? Or what is the issue?

 

The solution text states:

 

From now on, the system will be enhanced to use the Marital Status (T5UTK) table or the BSI Filing Status (T5UTKZ) table as source of information for valid filing statuses depending on customer configuration option BSI FILING STATUS SYNCHRONIZATION (BSIFS).
The valid filing statuses data is provided by BSI and there is no maintenance need on the new table.

To start using the new functionality after applying the main SAP Note and its prerequisites, it is mandatory to execute the Synchronize Payroll Tax Data report (RPUPAYUS_SYNC_TAX_DATA) to populate the new database table with the data provided by the BSI TaxFactory 11.0.

- New configuration option to activate/deactivate the usage of T5UTKZ (BSI filing status synchronized table) as source for valid filing statuses.

Configuration option BSI FILING STATUS SYNCHRONIZATION (BSIFS) was created in the Possible configuration options (V_T5F99K0) view to activate or deactivate the usage of T5UTKZ as source of information for valid filing statuses.

By default, SAP system will consider this option as Manual Filing Status (maint required) (OFF) and customers using BSIFS as OFF will have no impact on the current functionality of the system. Customers can set this option with Automatic Filing Status synchronization (ON) in Customer values for configuration options (V_T5F99K2) view to select the filing statuses source of information as the table synchronized with the BSI Tax Factory 11.0 API ReturnMaritalStatusInfoForFormula.

A summary of the new configuration option is shown below:

BSIFS OptionBehavior
OFF (Default)Manual Filing Status (maint required)
ON Automatic Filing Status synchronization

 

- New BAdI Definition for BSI Filing Status Descriptions (HRPAYUS_B_FLNG_STS_DESCRIPTION).

SAP delivers default BAdI implementation and example on class CL_HRPAYUS_IM_FLNG_STS_DESCRIP using descriptions for filing statuses from 1 to 25 based on BSI TaxFactory 11.0 documentation. These descriptions are only used by the new functionality when the BSIFS configuration is set to ON. Customers can customize any filing status description on BAdI HRPAYUS_B_FLNG_STS_DESCRIPTION following the default implementation.

The new BAdI has the T5UTKZ structure as importing parameter, which has the following fields:

  • Tax authority
  • Tax type
  • Formula number
  • Withholding format
  • Filing status
  • End date
  • Begin date
  • Default filing status

All these parameters can be used by customers to determine the BAdI output description which contains a long description (40 characters length) and a short description (10 characters length).

The View for BSI Filing Status table (V_T5UTKZ) is used to display the filing status values synchronized with the TaxFactory 11.0 combined with the BAdI's output long description.

Note 

For the states without withholding tax or without the filing status field in the state withholding certificate, BSI considers only the filing status 0 as a valid value. When creating a new 0210 infotype entry for one of these states, you may receive the message “No values found” when searching for valid filing statuses.
To use the new functionality for these states (BSIFS configuration option set to ON), you must hide or make optional the filing status field on the state infotype screen following the SAP Knowledge Base Article 2938426.

 

 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Financials Development

entry.title: How to find SAP APIs for SAP S/4HANA 3-tier extensibility model
entry.content:
If you want to develop a custom application in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition or SAP S/4HANA system using ABAP Cloud and the 3-tier extensibility model you will need to work with the SAP APIs to access the SAP standard in a clean core compatible way. This blog provides the overview of the relevant sources for looking for the SAP APIs, whereby all sources have the same content.

1. In the ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse

In the ABAP development tools for Eclipse create your own ABAP Repository Tree (context menu New->ABAP Repository Tree on the project node in the Project Explorer), choose "Released Objects" from the list in the Create Tree wizard, click the Next button, enter the name ("Released Objects") and click the Finish button. The Released Objects tree will be added to the Project Explorer: 

2. On the SAP Business Acceleration Hub

SAP Business Acceleration Hub is a SAP operated platform to explore, discover and consume APIs, pre-packaged integrations, business services and sample apps from SAP. You can look there for SAP APIs for the relevant SAP S/4HANA product:

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Cloud Edition
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Cloud Edition
SAP S/4HANA

3. On the GitHub

The Cloudification Repository on the Github provided by SAP offers you for the SAP S/4HANA products the relevant SAP APIs lists as .json and .csv files:

Cloudification Repository at SAP/abap-atc-cr-cv-s4hc (github.com)

4. In the Cloudification Viewer

You can use the Cloudification Viewer to access the SAP APIs lists from the Cloudification Repository in the comfortable way:

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS1
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Classic APIs

For details click on a single row to display additional content.
Using the Show Filter Bar button you can choose the relevant SAP APIs list for your SAP S/4HANA product.

4. External tooling

Software-Heroes

entry.title: Missing cache control directives in sap
entry.content:
Hi SAP,
As per Audit Security team in bank, There is requirement to set the cache control directives for SAP Fiori url and Solution manager java stack.Cache control value should be set as mentioned below.
Please find the attachment for the inexepected output from SAP url.Please help on how to set the cache control as per below in SAP.Recommended value :
cache-control: no-cache, nostore, must-revalidate, pre-check=0, post-check=0, max-age=0, s-maxage=0 pragma: no-cache
expires: -1

Regards

entry.title: Disable Create button using SAP RAP
entry.content:
Hello,

I am developing an app using restful ABAP where I have a parent entity as list report and child entity as line item (table) in the object page.
I am trying to disable the 'Create' button based on some conditions for the child entity. Following are the steps I followed.

1. In the projection view for child entity, I defined the following
@UI.createHidden: #(Hide_create)

2. Created a virtual field in the projection view
@ObjectModel.virtualElementCalculatedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_FIELD_CONFIG'
virtual Hide_create: abap_boolean

3. in class ZCL_FIELD_CONFIG, i am passing Hide_create = abap_true based on some conditions.

Here, the control is never going to the class ZCL_FIELD_CONFIG to calculate the Hide_create value.
But, if I give the annotation @UI.createHidden:true in the projection view, the create button is disabled.

Please let me know how can I disable it based on the condition.

entry.title: Re: Hiding / Disabling window in SAPScript
entry.content:
In case anyone is looking, this is how to remove a Window. Call just before the CLOSE_FORM function module:

CONSTANTS lc_page_windows(25) TYPE c VALUE '(SAPLSTXC)PAGE_WINDOWS[]'.
DATA: ls_page_windows TYPE itcth.
* =======================================================
ASSIGN (lc_page_windows) TO field-symbols(<fs_page_windows>).
IF <fs_page_windows> IS ASSIGNED.
	READ TABLE <fs_page_windows> INTO ls_page_windows WITH KEY tdwindow = 'window_name_here'.
	IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
		DELETE TABLE <fs_page_windows> FROM ls_page_windows.
	ENDIF.
ENDIF.

entry.title: Custom CDS View with several dependencies deprecated
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

The Primary Datasource of a CDS view is deprecated and we are having several CDS views that are dependent on this view that also have CDS views that are dependent on this. There is no way to just replace the primary data source without causing issues for the other CDS views?

 

Thanks!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Custom CDS View with several dependencies deprecated
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

Due to stability reasons it is not possible to change a used Custom CDS View in an incompatible way. Exchanging the primary data source would very likely break the dependent other Custom CDS Views and hence breaking your Key User extensibility scenario.

Therefore we propose to replace the now deprecated data source step-by-step: You can create a copy of the first Custom CDS View and replace the deprecated data source in that way. Then step-by-step you replace the original Custom CDS View with the copied one.

We know this approach might be cumbersome, but stability is one of the main criteria of Key User extensibility.

 

Best regards,

SAP Support.

entry.title: Re: Personas - hidden tabs still showing in SAPGui but ok...
entry.content:
Thanks for quick response, appreciate your help.

To clarify - We have hidden tabs in MM02.   However if you click the 'enter' key to move from one tab to the next, it stops on the hidden tab.

In Webgui - the tab is greyed out and a message shown 'this tab cannot be displayed'.   User must hit enter to move past this tab to the next one.   For example, we've hidden Accounting 2 tab.   User clicks enter from accounting 1, it shows accounting 2 with message 'this tab cannot be displayed', user clicks enter to move to Costing 1.

Ideally, if the tab is hidden, the flavour would automatically skip it, but as it's not, we created a script to click enter key on behalf of user and assigned it to the 'on enter' script event.    

Initially, it seems to be a personas issue, as would not expect to need script to skip the hidden tab.  However, if script is required, the script works in webgui, but when we test in sapgui and Business Client, the script does not work, behaviour is to ignore the script and show the hidden tabs. 

Script:

const Screens = [
[1,"wnd[0]/usr/tabsTABSPR1/tabpSP01/ssubTABFRA1:SAPLMGMM:2004/subSUB1:SAPLMGD1:1002/ctxtRMMG1-MATNR",session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/tabsTABSPR1/tabpSP01").visible],

function ScreenNumber() {
        for ( let i = 0; i< Screens.length;i++) {
            if (session.idExists(Screens[i][1])) {
            return [Screens[i][1].slice(29,31),i]
        }}return ["na",-1]
        }
function NextScreen() {



    var x = ScreenNumber()[1];
    var flag = true;
    var text = '';
    while (flag && x < Screens.length-1) {
        x += 1;

        if (Screens[x][2]) {
            flag = false;
            break;

        }

}

if (ScreenNumber()[1] == x){return false;} else{

if (!flag) {
session.findById(Screens[x][1].slice(0,31)).select()
}
return true;}
}

return NextScreen();

entry.title: Re: S4HANA ON PREMISE 1610 - Append structure to MSEG
entry.content:
We have to firstly create structure which can be used to append in MATDOC + MSEG

e.g. i created it for field SBATCH.

Now refer same structure & append it in MATDOC & MSEG

activate it

In MSEG do append structure but it won't get activated until we extend View NSDM_E_MSEG

Now finally create extension of view NSDM_E_MSEG

And activate extended view & mseg.

This will work.

entry.title: The Indispensable Role of SAP Basis Consultants in the Rise with SAP Era
entry.content:
In the ever-evolving landscape of enterprise technology, SAP remains a cornerstone for countless businesses worldwide. With the introduction of "Rise with SAP," a comprehensive offering aimed at helping businesses transform into intelligent enterprises, the need for skilled professionals has never been more critical. Among these professionals, SAP Basis consultants play a pivotal role in ensuring the seamless operation and optimization of SAP landscapes. Let's delve into why SAP Basis consultants are indispensable for businesses embarking on their journey with Rise with SAP.

Understanding SAP Basis:

Before delving into the significance of SAP Basis consultants in the Rise with SAP era, it's essential to grasp the role of SAP Basis itself. SAP Basis forms the foundation for all SAP applications, managing the technical infrastructure and providing support for various modules and functionalities. From installation and configuration to performance monitoring and system maintenance, SAP Basis consultants are responsible for keeping SAP environments running smoothly.

### Rise with SAP: A Transformational Journey:

Rise with SAP represents a paradigm shift in how businesses approach their digital transformation journey. It offers a holistic solution encompassing business process optimization, cloud migration, and intelligent technologies like AI and machine learning. As organizations embrace this transformative approach, the role of SAP Basis consultants becomes even more crucial.

The Need for SAP Basis Consultants in Rise with SAP:

1. Infrastructure Management: With Rise with SAP's focus on cloud migration and infrastructure modernization, SAP Basis consultants are instrumental in architecting, deploying, and managing cloud-based SAP environments. Their expertise ensures the efficient utilization of cloud resources and the seamless integration of on-premises and cloud systems.

2. Performance Optimization:As businesses leverage advanced technologies to enhance their operations, SAP Basis consultants play a vital role in optimizing system performance. They fine-tune configurations, monitor resource utilization, and implement best practices to ensure optimal performance and scalability, thereby maximizing the benefits of Rise with SAP.

3. Security and Compliance: In an era of increasing cybersecurity threats and regulatory requirements, SAP Basis and Security consultants are tasked with safeguarding SAP systems against vulnerabilities and ensuring compliance with industry standards. They implement robust security measures, conduct regular audits, and provide guidance on data protection, helping organizations mitigate risks and protect their sensitive information.

4. Integration and Innovation:Rise with SAP emphasizes the integration of SAP solutions with third-party applications and emerging technologies. SAP Basis consultants facilitate seamless integration by configuring interfaces, managing middleware components, and collaborating with developers to enable innovation and unlock new possibilities for businesses.

Conclusion:

As businesses embark on their journey with Rise with SAP, the role of SAP Basis consultants emerges as indispensable. From infrastructure management to performance optimization and security, SAP Basis consultants form the backbone of SAP operations, enabling organizations to unleash the full potential of their digital transformation initiatives. In the dynamic landscape of enterprise technology, investing in skilled SAP Basis talent is not just a necessity but a strategic imperative for success in the Rise with SAP era

entry.title: Seamlessly Integrating SAPUI5 with SAP Backend Systems: Empowering Modern Web Applications with Ente
entry.content:
Understanding SAPUI5

Before delving into integration with SAP backend systems, let's briefly understand SAPUI5. SAPUI5 is a comprehensive UI development toolkit for building web applications that adhere to SAP's Fiori design principles. It provides a rich set of pre-built UI controls, data binding capabilities, and robust support for responsive design, making it an ideal choice for developing enterprise-grade applications.

Integration with SAP ERP

SAP ERP serves as the backbone of many organizations, managing core business processes such as finance, supply chain, and human resources. SAPUI5 seamlessly integrates with SAP ERP through various mechanisms such as OData services, RFC (Remote Function Call), and BAPIs (Business Application Programming Interfaces). Developers can leverage SAPUI5's data binding capabilities to consume data from SAP ERP and display it within web applications. Additionally, SAPUI5 offers built-in support for SAP's authentication mechanisms, ensuring secure communication between the front-end and backend systems.

Leveraging SAP S/4HANA

SAP S/4HANA, SAP's next-generation ERP suite, provides real-time analytics, predictive capabilities, and streamlined processes to drive digital transformation. SAPUI5 is fully compatible with SAP S/4HANA, allowing developers to build intuitive user interfaces that leverage the advanced features of the platform. With SAPUI5's support for Fiori elements, developers can create consistent and visually appealing applications that align with SAP's Fiori design guidelines. Furthermore, SAPUI5's modular architecture enables easy extensibility, allowing developers to enhance existing S/4HANA applications with custom functionality.

Integration with SAP Cloud Platform

SAP Cloud Platform serves as a robust platform-as-a-service (PaaS) offering from SAP, providing developers with a comprehensive set of tools and services for building, deploying, and managing cloud-based applications. SAPUI5 seamlessly integrates with SAP Cloud Platform, enabling developers to build scalable and resilient web applications that leverage cloud-based services such as SAP HANA, SAP Fiori Launchpad, and SAP Business Application Studio. With SAPUI5's support for multi-environment deployment, developers can deploy applications to SAP Cloud Platform with ease, ensuring seamless integration with SAP backend systems.

Best Practices for Integration

When integrating SAPUI5 with SAP backend systems, it's essential to follow best practices to ensure optimal performance, security, and maintainability. Some best practices include:

  • Use asynchronous programming techniques to prevent blocking UI threads and improve responsiveness.
  • Implement error handling and exception logging to gracefully handle communication errors and unexpected behavior.
  • Utilize caching mechanisms to reduce round-trip times and improve application performance.
  • Follow SAP's security guidelines for authentication, authorization, and data encryption to protect sensitive information.

Conclusion

SAPUI5 provides developers with a powerful toolkit for building modern web applications that seamlessly integrate with SAP backend systems such as SAP ERP, SAP S/4HANA, and SAP Cloud Platform. By leveraging SAPUI5's rich feature set and best practices for integration, developers can unlock the full potential of enterprise data, empowering organizations to drive innovation and achieve business success in today's digital economy.

entry.title: Understanding SAP CPQ: Streamlining Configure, Price, and Quote Processes
entry.content:
Introduction:
In today's highly competitive business landscape, companies are continuously seeking ways to optimize their sales processes to stay ahead. One critical aspect of this optimization is the Configure, Price, and Quote (CPQ) process, which directly impacts sales efficiency and effectiveness. SAP CPQ (Configure, Price, and Quote) emerges as a robust solution tailored to address the complexities of modern sales environments. In this technical blog, we delve into SAP CPQ, exploring its features, functionalities, benefits, and implementation considerations.

What is SAP CPQ?
SAP CPQ is a cloud-based solution designed to streamline the Configure, Price, and Quote processes for businesses across various industries. It empowers organizations to create accurate quotes, configure complex products/services, and optimize pricing strategies to drive sales performance.

Key Features of SAP CPQ:

1. Product Configuration: SAP CPQ enables users to configure products/services according to customer requirements, considering various attributes, options, and dependencies. This feature ensures accurate configurations, minimizing errors and maximizing customer satisfaction.

2. Dynamic Pricing: With SAP CPQ, businesses can implement dynamic pricing strategies based on factors such as customer segments, competitive pricing analysis, discounts, promotions, and market trends. This flexibility helps organizations remain competitive while maximizing profitability.

3. Quote Management:The platform offers robust capabilities for quote creation, management, and customization. Users can generate professional-looking quotes quickly, incorporating relevant product information, pricing details, terms, and conditions.

4. Integration Capabilities: SAP CPQ seamlessly integrates with other enterprise systems such as CRM (Customer Relationship Management), ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning), and e-commerce platforms. This integration ensures data consistency, enhances collaboration, and provides a unified view of customer interactions across the sales cycle.

5. Analytics and Reporting: The solution provides comprehensive analytics and reporting functionalities, offering insights into sales performance, quote-to-cash metrics, customer preferences, and profitability analysis. These insights empower organizations to make data-driven decisions and continuously improve their sales strategies.

Benefits of SAP CPQ:

1. Improved Sales Efficiency: By automating and streamlining the CPQ process, SAP CPQ reduces manual effort, minimizes errors, and accelerates quote generation. This efficiency allows sales teams to focus more on value-added activities such as customer engagement and relationship building.

2. Enhanced Customer Experience: SAP CPQ enables organizations to deliver personalized quotes quickly, based on customer preferences and requirements. This personalized approach enhances the overall customer experience, fostering loyalty and satisfaction.

3. Optimized Pricing Strategies: With dynamic pricing capabilities, SAP CPQ helps businesses optimize pricing strategies to remain competitive while maximizing profitability. Organizations can adapt pricing in real-time based on market dynamics, customer feedback, and business objectives.

4. Scalability and Flexibility:As a cloud-based solution, SAP CPQ offers scalability and flexibility to accommodate evolving business needs and growth requirements. Whether expanding into new markets or adding new product lines, the platform can adapt to changes seamlessly.

5. Compliance and Governance: SAP CPQ provides robust compliance and governance features, ensuring adherence to regulatory requirements, pricing policies, and contractual obligations. This reduces compliance risks and enhances overall business integrity.

Implementation Considerations:

1. Business Process Alignment: Before implementing SAP CPQ, organizations should align the solution with their existing sales processes and workflows. This alignment ensures smooth integration and adoption across the organization.

2. Data Migration and Integration: Proper data migration and integration planning are essential for seamless connectivity between SAP CPQ and other enterprise systems. This includes mapping data fields, ensuring data consistency, and implementing data validation rules.

3. User Training and Adoption: Comprehensive training programs should be conducted to familiarize users with SAP CPQ's features, functionalities, and best practices. User adoption is critical for maximizing the ROI of the solution.

4. Continuous Improvement: Post-implementation, organizations should continuously monitor and evaluate the effectiveness of SAP CPQ. This includes gathering feedback from users, analyzing performance metrics, and identifying areas for optimization and enhancement.

Conclusion:
SAP CPQ emerges as a powerful solution for organizations seeking to streamline their Configure, Price, and Quote processes. By leveraging its advanced features, businesses can enhance sales efficiency, improve customer experience, optimize pricing strategies, and drive overall sales performance. However, successful implementation requires careful planning, alignment with business processes, and a commitment to continuous improvement. With SAP CPQ, organizations can navigate the complexities of modern sales environments with confidence and agility.

entry.title: Re: Application and system - a worthless debate?
entry.content:
I have had the same debate when it comes to SLA discussions... is that system availabilty or application availabilty ?
At the end of the day a system is an instance of an application with a unique name or system identifier (SID). Each of the systems that make up a given  SAP landscape is part of an environment or a tier like SBX, Dev, QAS, Pre-prod, Prod etc..

entry.title: CFOP Wrong determination MM
entry.content:
HI,

I have a question regarding the determination of CFOP in J_1BAON, We have a Non-sap integration with SAP that generates a Purchase order and its MIRO invoice for a freight service by BAPI.

The NFe created in J1B1N has partners such as provider (LF), payer (RG), merchant receiver (WE) among others.
In the determination of the CFOP for the material it is taking an incorrect path as it is determining that the freight is within the same state by the configuration of the supplier's master data and determines CFOP 1353, however we require that you take the configuration of the merchandise receiver that go outside the state to determine CFOP 2353.

We require to know if there is any configuration to indicate the J_1B_NF_CFOP_2_DETERMINATION function that the destination category must be taken from the merchandise receiver that is in a different state or to know if there is any function to set the correct CFOP without taking the standard determination.

 

thanks

entry.title: Introduce more about linking vendor and customer to same Business Partner
entry.content:
During migrate your system from ECC to HANA, we need to synchronize all customer and vendor to BP. One of the situation is if the customer and vendor are same entity, we need to link them to same Business Partner.

Error R1367

In the past, we always receive below error:

Consistency error: 
Message no. R1367

This is because our system is checking below:

        a. The Customer and Vendor must be linked through the Customer number field in the Vendor table and the Vendor number field in the customer table.
        b. If the Customer and Vendor have the same Bank Account information (same Country, Bank Key and Bank Account), then the remaining fields for this entry must also match.
        c. The customer and vendor must contain exactly the same Postal Address information.The following fields are also checked :

ADDR_VERSPO_W_O_NOLANGUPCODE1_EXT
FROM_DATEPO_BOX_CITLANGU_ISOPCODE2_EXT
TO_DATEPBOXCIT_NOREGIONPCODE3_EXT
TITLEPO_BOX_REGSORT1PO_BOX
NAMEPOBOX_CTRYSORT2FLOOR
NAME_2PO_CTRYISOEXTENS_1ROOM_NO
NAME_3DELIV_DISEXTENS_2COUNTRY
NAME_4TRANSPZONETIME_ZONECOUNTRYISO
CONV_NAMESTREETTAXJURCODEDELI_SERV_TYPE
C_O_NAMESTREET_NOADDRESS_IDDELI_SERV_NUMBER
CITYSTR_ABBRLANGU_CRCOUNTY_CODE
DISTRICTHOUSE_NOLANGUCRISOCOUNTY
CITY_NOHOUSE_NO2COMM_TYPETOWNSHIP_CODE
DISTRCT_NOHOUSE_NO3ADDR_GROUPTOWNSHIP
CHCKSTATUSSTR_SUPPL1HOME_CITY 
REGIOGROUPSTR_SUPPL2HOMECITYNO 
POSTL_COD1STR_SUPPL3DONT_USE_S 
POSTL_COD2LOCATIONDONT_USE_P 
POSTL_COD3BUILDINGPO_BOX_LOBBY 

If any of above does not meet the requirement, error will occurs.

Improvement of error R1367

Then we have below note which improve the error message.

3088419 - DDIC for Legal Entity BAdI Message (367) Enhancements   

3067086 - Legal Entity BAdI Message (367) Enhancements   

The error message will show in below way:

CVI_LEDH

We also develop a functionality so that we can select the leading object instead of manually change every difference field between customer and vendor.

To realize this functionality, please implement below note :

 3072319 - Legal Entity - Data Harmonization (CVI_LE_DATA_HARMONIZATION) Report

 3088771 - DDIC Changes for Legal Entity - Data Harmonization (CVI_LE_DATA_HARMONIZATION) Report

 3082146 - Legal Entity - Data Harmonization (CVI_LE_DATA_HARMONIZATION) Report integration in CVI_CO...

 3081338 - Legal Entity - Data Harmonization (CVI_LE_DATA_HARMONIZATION) Report integration into MDS...

 Then let us look into CVI_LEDH

  • The difference between customer and vendor will get checked in t-code CVI_LEDH, it including address check, tax check, and bank check.

  • If both of the customer and vendor does not synchronize to BP yet, they will get shown in the result of CVI_LEDH.
  • We can select the leading object, which means during synchronize from customer and vendor to BP, the BP will use the data from customer or from vendor. For example, for the sample customer MYCUST71 and vendor MYVEND71, I select customer as leading object for address data, and vendor as leading object for bank data.
  • After selecting, please press save button.

Let us see what will happen during synchronization:

Sample customer MYCUST71, does not maintain bank data.

Sample vendor MYVEND71, maintain one bank data.

And the synchronization options set up in below way.

  • After vendor synchronize to BP, currently the customer does not synchronize to BP. Re-run the t-code, the customer MYCUST71 and vendor MYVEND71 (LIEF) will no longer there.

  • If checking into table CVI_LEDH, we can get below record:

  • Bp has the bank data which get synchronized from vendor

  • Run MDS_LOAD_Cockpit to synchronize customer to BP. The BP then has both customer role and vendor role. And bank data keep no changes.
     

     

  • Customer still does not have bank data

  • At this time, when looking into table CVI_LEDH, the status keeps 03.

     

  • Then I re-run t-code CVI_LEDH, the status change to 05

     

  • Let us see if we change any data at customer side. 

    BP name get changed, but the bank data keeps


     

     

  • If we try to add a new bank data to customer. After it synchronize to BP, it behaves below

     

 Conclusion

You can see that after using functionality CVI_LEDH, if there are difference between customer and vendor, we can select one of it as leading object, after both of them get synchronized to BP, it will not change the data which didn't get selected as leading object. In this case, vendor has same bank information as BP, but customer still keep no bank data.

Please also follow below steps, to make sure customer, vendor and BP data are same.

MDS_LOAD_COCKPIT: Business Partner -> Customer
a. Select only Business Partners where customer and vendor are merged into.
b. This step is to synchronize the address, tax data, and bank details of the Business
Partner to the Customer.
c. This step is also required to link the previous vendor Contact Person to customer

MDS_LOAD_COCKPIT: Business Partner -> Vendor
a. Select only Business Partners where customer and vendor are merged into.
b. This step is to synchronize the address, tax data, and bank details of the Business
Partner to the Vendor.
c. This step is also required to link the previous customer Contact Person to vendor

Best regards

sherry

entry.title: Manage SAP HANA Cloud instance on BTP using BTP CLI
entry.content:
SAP HANA Cloud instances (other environments) can be created and managed using the BTP CLI.

This blog is summarizing the common operations.

Please note: SAP HANA Cloud instances provisioned in the cloud foundry environment cannot be managed by BTP CLI at the time of writing. 

Check my other blog for managing instances in cloud foundry environment.
Manage SAP HANA Cloud instance on BTP using Cloud Foundry CLI 

Prerequisite:

  • Install the BTP CLI on your local machine
  • HANA Cloud Entitlement added in the subaccount
  • Subscribed the hana tools in your subaccount (optional)
  • Use BTP Login to authenticate
  • Use BTP Target to set the default subaccount target

Create HANA Cloud instance (in other environments)

In free tier or production, choose offering name as "hana-cloud". In trial, choose "hana-cloud-trial".

In free tier, choose plan name as "hana-free". In trial or production, choose "hana".

Bash/Shell:

 

 

btp create services/instance --offering-name hana-cloud --plan-name hana-free --name test-db --parameters '{"data": {"memory": 32, "systempassword": "Welcome100."}}'

 

 

 It will start provisioning an instance in "other environment" and soon the new service instance will be visible from BTP cockpit namely "test-db". 
Alternatively, check the service using the BTP CLI, copy the instance id for future reference.

 

 

btp list services/instance

 

 

or

 

 

btp get services/instance --name test-db

 

 

Start/Stop an instance

To stop:
Bash/Shell:

 

 

btp update services/instance --id 0e269e34-0bb3-47eb-b09b-cc5050dcb3cc --parameters '{"data":{"serviceStopped":true}}'

 

 

To start:
Bash/Shell:

 

 

 

btp update services/instance --id 0e269e34-0bb3-47eb-b09b-cc5050dcb3cc --parameters '{"data":{"serviceStopped":false}}'

 

 

Update whitelisted IPs

For local/hybrid or cross-region developers, whitelisted IPs are needed to be updated on the instance level for connections.

To open connections from all IPs.
Bash/Shell:

 

 

btp update services/instance --id 0e269e34-0bb3-47eb-b09b-cc5050dcb3cc --parameters '{"data":{"whitelistIPs":["0.0.0.0/0"]}}'

 

 

 

Full list of the supported parameters can be found here.

Delete an instance

Bash/Shell:

 

btp delete services/instance --id 0e269e34-0bb3-47eb-b09b-cc5050dcb3cc

 

Limitations:

  • BTP CLI is not able to manage instances created in Cloud Foundry runtime environment at the time of writing. Cloud Foundry CLI is the command line tool for HANA Cloud instances inside the cloud foundry runtime. See my blog here for details.
  • Instance created using BTP CLI is in "other environments" and able to be managed via the SAP HANA Cloud Central, multi-environment edition.
  • BTP CLI can only be used in subaccount which feature B is enabled.

BTP CLI command reference can be found here.

entry.title: Add multiple entries to Outbound queue in SMQ1
entry.content:
Greetings all,

In ECC, I implemented the logic that creating an outbound queue ( which can check in tcode SMQ1 or table TRFCQOUT) and triggers the scheduling of a background job for this queue with a 5-munites delay. Upon the re-execution of this logic within the 5-munites, additional entries are appended to this queue name. 

The primary objective of this implementation is to streamline the process by consolidating multiple entries under a single background job. It helps to improve the performance by minimize the number of background job running parallel at the same time. 

Following the example of below sap document, everything is working fine in ECC system.

However, It doesn't working properly in S4 HANA system. Within 5-munite delay time, if this logic was executed again, then this queue name has gone immediately (cannot add a 3rd entry) and the background job is removed (cannot see it in SM37). 

I am still struggling with this issue 😞 and uncertain if there are any configurations for background jobs in S4 Hana system that could provide assistance 

https://help.sap.com/doc/saphelp_nw73ehp1/7.31.19/en-us/48/bb8094b22337e3e10000000a42189d/content.htm?loaded_from_frameset=true

 

Here is the logic implementing: 

 

* Get the latest entry of outbound queue into ls_qout (can use FM TRFC_QOUT_OVERVIEW_STATES)
* Based on ls_qout, we can get the start time of the latest queue (ls_qout-ftime)
* in order to know this queue is still deplaying for 5-munite or not 
IF ls_qout IS NOT INITIAL.
  IF ls_qout-ftime + 300 > sy-uzeit. "300 seconds = 5 munites
    lv_qname = ls_qout-qname
  ENDIF.
ENDIF.
 
* Set the new queue name if it cannot find any queue which is still deplaying
* And also set the flag lv_schedule_backgroundjob = 'X'
IF lv_qname IS INITIAL.
  lv_qname = 'assign_the_queue_name'.
  lv_schedule_backgroundjob = 'X'.
ENDIF.
 
* Initializing the TRFC Queue
CALL FUNCTION 'TRFC_QUEUE_INITIALIZE' .
 
* Set the queue name for the outbound queue
 CALL FUNCTION 'TRFC_SET_QUEUE_NAME'
    EXPORTING
      qname  = lv_qname
      nosend = space.
 
* Call tRFC without destination because I need it running locally in this system
 CALL FUNCTION 'rfc_function'
    IN BACKGROUND TASK ...
 
* Schedule the background job starts in next 5 munites for only the first one execution
IF lv_schedule_backgroundjob = 'X'.
        lv_start_date = sy-datum.
        lv_start_time = sy-uzeit + 300.
 
        CALL FUNCTION 'START_OF_BACKGROUNDTASK'
          EXPORTING
            startdate = lv_start_date
            starttime = lv_start_time.
 
ENDIF.
 
* At the time of the COMMIT WORK, the qRFC manager creates a counter for the LUW
  COMMIT WORK.
